mappings.reason: Addressing unauthorized software by removing it from use directly satisfies CFM-05's requirement to disable prohibited client run time technologies.
Allowlisting authorized software ensures only approved software executes, effectively disabling all prohibited technologies as required by CFM-05.
Allowlisting authorized libraries prevents unauthorized libraries from loading, addressing a specific subset of client run time technologies.
Allowlisting authorized scripts prevents unauthorized scripts from executing, addressing a specific subset of client run time technologies.
Uninstalling or disabling unnecessary services addresses a subset of client run time technologies (services) that should be prohibited if not required.
Ensuring only supported browsers and email clients execute disables prohibited (unsupported) client applications, a subset of run time technologies.
Restricting unauthorized browser and email client extensions disables prohibited client run time components, a subset of the broader technology scope.
Disabling autorun and autoplay functionality disables a specific prohibited client run time technology feature, addressing a subset of the requirement.
